TextENGNew: The van pulled into the shrine's parking lot.
...Parked there were a number of vans fitted with loudspeakers on their roofs -- makeshift propaganda vehicles.

Scrawled across every one of them were brush-stroke slogans: 'Crush the Dam Project!'@k 'Defend Our Homeland to the Death!'@k 'Know the Wrath of Oyashiro-sama!'@k Even without the loudspeakers, they projected an aura intimidating enough to cow any onlooker.
Vertical banners had been erected everywhere.
...Their contents were much the same.
Anti-dam protests and crude denunciations of the government.@kBanners, placards, and more...
...There was no mistaking it. This was the headquarters of the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance.
changeReason: New translation renders 死守同盟 as 'Defense Alliance' instead of 'Guardians,' better conveying the activist nature of the group.

TextENGNew: But even in such a charged place, for Rika-chan... this was also her home.
changeReason: New translation uses 'charged' instead of 'hectic' for 物騒, better conveying the sense of danger/volatility.

TextENGNew: ``...We've arrived.@k@vS05/27/100500032. I'm home.``
Rika-chan clambered over my lap and was the first out of the vehicle.
...Makino noticed that my gaze had caught on the anti-dam banners.
``The shrine also serves as the office for the anti-dam movement.@k@vS05/00/104100068. For someone your age, this must feel nostalgic -- like the old anti-security treaty protests! Heh heh heh!``
...There was a time when opposing government policies through quasi-terrorist means -- students seizing buildings, clashing with riot police -- was practically a trend.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Students barricading themselves in lecture halls, brawling with the riot squads that stormed in...
There was an era when squandering your youth on such things was seen as the most glorious path a man could walk.@k...It was slightly before my time, though.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``I'm no activist, personally.@k@vS05/13/101300203. I studied without incident and graduated without incident.@k@vS05/13/101300204. I don't believe violence or occupations should be used to oppose government policy.``
``...Oh?@k@vS05/00/104100070. ...Well now, that's a fine opinion to hold...``
``...If you disagree with a policy, you should either vote for a politician who shares your view, or enter the political arena yourself and make your case through legitimate channels.``
``That is the foundation of democracy.@k@vS05/13/101300207. ...Challenging it with violence is something that should never be tolerated.``
For a young person these days... he holds quite admirable views.
...I thought I could see that kind of respect reflected in Makino's gaze.
...Just then, I felt a tug at my sleeve.
``......I have absolutely no idea what Akasaka is talking about.``
Rika-chan, feeling left out of a conversation she couldn't follow, wore a look of displeasure.@k...That expression was so charming I had to fight to keep the corners of my mouth from curling upward.
They say fathers are always pushovers for their daughters... I was starting to understand that feeling.

``Sorry about that.@k@vS05/13/101300209. ...That was a bit too difficult for you, wasn't it, Rika-chan?@k@vS05/13/101300210. ...Japan is a peaceful country, so you have to express what you want to say through peaceful means.@k@vS05/13/101300211. You shouldn't resort to violence to make your point.``
...No amount of explaining would get through to a girl who proudly boasted about being able to do addition with carrying.@k...Perhaps this wasn't the sort of topic to discuss in front of Rika-chan.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``...So then, Akasaka.@k@vS05/27/100500035. ...How can we keep our village from being swallowed by the dam?``
Even if I regretted my careless words, it was too late.
...I was standing in the headquarters of an organization willing to force its demands through any means necessary...
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: And here I was, eloquently condemning the very use of violence to advance a cause...
It was only now that I noticed the complicated mix of emotions lurking alongside the respect in Makino's eyes...
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...This wasn't a matter of pretty ideals.
Anyone could see that what I'd said was technically correct.
Everyone knew that resorting to violence to push your agenda was nothing to be proud of.
...But right now... Hinamizawa was in a predicament that peaceful protest alone might not be able to overcome.
The legitimacy of the Hinamizawa Dam Project was being shaken by the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance's various media campaigns.
changeReason: New translation renders 死守同盟 as 'Defense Alliance' instead of 'Guardians,' better conveying the activist nature of the group.

TextENGNew: ...If they intensified their efforts further... they might succeed in freezing or altering the construction plans.
But... that was only a possibility, nothing more.
If the government proceeded with construction on schedule, this place would be at the bottom of a lake within a few years.
...Were it not for the villagers' desperate, all-out resistance, it might already be underwater.
``...We can only live here.@k@vS05/27/100500037. ...We can't survive in the city.``
...This single remark from a girl who was still so clearly a child... spoke for the innermost cries of Hinamizawa's residents with devastating precision.
Of course... there was nothing I could say in response.
...Makino, too, could only fall silent in the face of Rika-chan's weighty words...
``...I'm sorry.@k@vS05/13/101300213. ...This isn't something an outsider should speak lightly about.@k@vS05/13/101300214. ...I apologize.``
Makino, unsure how to respond for a while, could only laugh awkwardly.
``Heh... heh heh!@k@vS05/00/104100072. It's nothing that concerns folks who live somewhere else.``
``If you've taken a liking to Hinamizawa... please tell all your friends what a wonderful place it is.``
``If word spreads to dozens, maybe even hundreds of people... then that absurd plan to sink this village under a reservoir will eventually collapse on its own.``
changeReason: New translation corrects 'at the foot of a dam' to 'under a reservoir,' accurately describing submersion by the dam's reservoir.

TextENGNew: Then, with a reassuring laugh, he told me not to worry about it.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...It was truly fortunate that Makino didn't take it too hard.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...Getting caught up in befriending a little girl had made me forget the tension of being in enemy territory entirely.@k...Perhaps it was a good thing I was reminded of it just before setting foot in their headquarters.
If we'd had that exchange inside their office...
...If there had been a hot-tempered young man in there, he might have come at me.
...Given how radical they could be... in the worst case, my life could have been in danger.
...I must have looked quite despondent.
Makino, thinking he had inadvertently said something wrong, grew flustered trying to cheer me up...
``...I spoke carelessly just now.@k@vS05/13/101300216. I'm truly sorry.``
``No, no...@k@vS05/00/104100076. ...Please don't worry about it...``
``Every place you showed me around the village today was beautiful and wonderful.@k@vS05/13/101300218. ...You've truly made me want to come back again.``
``...If such a beautiful village is going to be submerged at the bottom of a lake... I can understand why you would fight so desperately.``
...I leveraged my crestfallen appearance to steer the conversation in a more favorable direction...
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Before long, through some careful maneuvering, Makino had regained his good spirits. He seemed convinced that I genuinely sympathized with their cause.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``Now then!@k@vS05/00/104100078. Let's leave that talk behind.@k@vS05/00/104100079. There's a place here with a truly wonderful view!``
``...Let's go, Akasaka.``
Rika-chan, seemingly delighted to be free of the gloomy adult conversation, bounded up the stone steps.
There was no need to run, but wanting to keep pace with her, I jogged lightly up the stairs...
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: The shrine grounds were dotted with several town council tents.
Inside the tents were folding tables and pipe chairs, where a number of elderly council members sat chatting animatedly.

If not for the sashes they wore reading 'Crush the Dam Project!' and other militant slogans, it would have looked like nothing more than a relaxed chat among neighborhood elders.@k...That's how peaceful the scene was.
changeReason: Key reversal: old says they WEREN'T saying incendiary things; JPN says sashes DID have militant slogans. Real correction but similar spirit.

TextENGNew: ...Beside the entrance of a shed that served as a meeting hall, a large sign was propped up, bearing the words 'Onigafuchi Defense Alliance Headquarters' in impressively bold brushwork.
changeReason: New translation renders 死守同盟 as 'Defense Alliance' instead of 'Guardians,' better conveying the activist nature of the group.

TextENGNew: This was... the headquarters of the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance.
...Honestly... it was somewhat anticlimactic.
If you asked what I'd been imagining...@kIt's a bit embarrassing, but I'd pictured something far more ominous.@k...Razor wire, barbed-wire barricades, that sort of thing.
But my first impression was completely different from that... it was just a backwater town council meeting hall on the grounds of a rural shrine.@k...That's all it looked like.
The old folks who'd been chatting noticed Rika-chan's arrival.
``Well, if it isn't Rika-chama.@k@vS05/00/oldB00002. It's time for little kids to be heading home!``
``...This is my home.``
The old men burst into laughter, since she wasn't wrong.
Even from that brief exchange, I could tell that this girl, Furude Rika, was adored by everyone.
``Makino-san, is that the person?@k@vS05/18/103300002. The tourist who phoned?``
``That's right.@k@vS05/00/104100081. Sir, this is the village chief. Mayor Kimiyoshi of Hinamizawa.``
changeReason: New translation uses 'village chief' for 村長, more accurately reflecting the rural administrative role.

TextENGNew: He smiled warmly... but there was no mistaking that he'd be formidable when angered. This affable old man was, in fact, none other than the president of the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance himself -- Kiichirou Kimiyoshi...
changeReason: New translation renders 死守同盟 as 'Defense Alliance' instead of 'Guardians,' better conveying the activist nature of the group.

TextENGNew: ``Welcome to Hinamizawa.@k@vS05/18/103300004. How did you find it?@k@vS05/18/103300005. ...It's a quiet, relaxing place, isn't it?``
``Y-Yes!@k@vS05/13/101300221. I was able to enjoy many beautiful locations throughout the day.@k@vS05/13/101300222. I'm deeply grateful for this rare opportunity.``
``...My...@k@vS05/18/103300007. Even though you're a youngster, you're quite the gentleman...@k@vS05/18/103300008. Mmhmm.``
``...Kiichirou.@k@vS05/27/100500041. I want to show Akasaka the place with the best view.``
Perhaps thinking the pleasantries between the mayor and me were dragging on, Rika-chan cut in.
...Calling even the village chief by his first name, no less.@k...It struck me as slightly odd, but none of the villagers seemed to mind.
changeReason: New translation uses 'village chief' for 村長, more accurately reflecting the rural administrative role.

TextENGNew: ...A girl of the Furude household, one of the Three Families.
...Perhaps she existed on a different plane from the ordinary villagers.
``...Akasaka, this way.``
Rika-chan tugged at my sleeve.
I didn't resist, letting myself be pulled along...@k...and there, a magnificent vista awaited me.
``...This is... incredible...``
From that hilltop vantage point, you could look down upon the entire village... a truly breathtaking panorama.
They knew this was the finest view in the whole village, and they had the grace not to break the spell while I stood there, awestruck and speechless.
...I was holding a camera, and yet I'd completely forgotten to even look through the viewfinder.
No matter what kind of film I printed this view on... it could never capture the whole of it.
If there was any way to convey it... it would only be through my own words.
How breathtakingly beautiful it was, how irreplaceable... the only thing I could do was try to express that feeling of wonder...
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: For a long while... captivated by the scenery... I could only stand there, lost in awe.
A cool breeze soothed my flushed body.@k...That pleasant sensation, too, held me spellbound...
Rika-chan, who had been holding on to my sleeve the whole time, spoke.
``...This is my very favorite place.``
As she said that, she showed me that 'nipah' smile once more.
That smile seemed terribly fragile.
...Because the view she loved so much from her favorite spot... would one day be swallowed by the dark green waters of a reservoir.
``...I just can't believe... that a village like this is going to end up at the bottom of a lake.``
I said it softly, so only Rika-chan could hear.
...And I regretted it the moment the words left my mouth.@k...What a... cruel thing to say.
But Rika-chan's expression didn't cloud over.
...If anything, she smiled back at me as she replied.
``...It won't sink.@k@vS05/27/100500045. The dam project will definitely be called off.``
Words without basis -- the kind only a little girl would utter.
...But her words carried something beyond logic.@k...They held a wish.
``...Akasaka... do you think this village will really be submerged?``
``...I don't want it to be.``
...I understood the importance of the dam.
I knew it was necessary.
...But I didn't want to accept the sacrifices it demanded.
I knew my stance was the classic 'agree in principle, oppose in practice'...
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: But... if it meant preserving this view that this girl loved so much...@k...I might be willing to oppose the dam.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...Casting aside the nation's interests, Japan's development, all for one girl's smile.@k...A fine display of humanism, perhaps, but...@k...............@k...Sentimentality, I suppose...
``...It won't sink.@k@vS05/27/100500048. Because the dam project will be gone very soon.``
Her words didn't carry the tone of hope or wishful thinking... but rather something akin to certainty.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: As if she were recounting a fate already sealed... unwavering.
Like stating the obvious -- that the sun would rise from the east tomorrow morning.
Wanting to ask the girl what grounds she had for saying that... I turned to face her.
``What makes you... so certain?``
She seemed to want to answer, but... as though unable to find the right words, she swallowed back what she was about to say.
``...Whether Akasaka does anything or not.@k@vS05/27/100500054. The dam project will end this year.@k@vS05/27/100500055. It's already been decided.``
That was a lie.
If the cancellation of the dam project were already decided, the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance would have ceased operations.
changeReason: New translation renders 死守同盟 as 'Defense Alliance' instead of 'Guardians,' better conveying the activist nature of the group.

TextENGNew: From what I could see, they looked like nothing other than people throwing themselves into an endless battle.
If they knew the dam project was canceled... the atmosphere here would be far more relaxed.@k...But this village was anything but relaxed.
...I only had to recall the hostile checkpoint, or the bus shelter bristling with that same energy.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...The villagers were still fighting, prepared to go down swinging.
And yet this girl, fully aware of the villagers' grim determination...@k...had already declared their victory as a foregone conclusion.
Villagers hurling themselves into an endless war, and a girl who calmly proclaimed they had already won.@k...The contrast was... profoundly strange.
``...Why do you believe that?@k@vS05/13/101300229. If you have some reason to be so certain, won't you tell me...?``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Rika-chan fell silent for a while, as if choosing her words carefully... then said, in a small voice--
``...Because.``
An expression that said she wasn't sure whether she should continue.
...As if she had started something she wasn't sure she was allowed to finish... that kind of expression.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``...It's already been decided.@k@vS05/27/100500058. ...I don't know how else to put it.``
``Y-you keep saying it's been decided, but...``
...Groundless words, the kind only a child would say.
...That's what I'd dismissed them as before, but... I could no longer be so sure.
...She was the only one speaking with such unwavering conviction.
...My real mission -- the one I had nearly forgotten amid this enjoyable day -- surfaced in the back of my mind.@k...The kidnapping of the minister's grandson...
Had the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance kidnapped the minister's grandson... and already succeeded in secret negotiations with the minister... securing a firm commitment to cancel the dam project...?
changeReason: New translation renders 死守同盟 as 'Defense Alliance' instead of 'Guardians,' better conveying the activist nature of the group.

TextENGNew: ...Had Kimiyoshi and the others actually known all along, merely feigning ignorance?
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...And was this girl innocently revealing it all to me...?
``...Akasaka.``
The girl called my name without warning.
``...What is it?``
``......Go back to Tokyo.``
What...?
It wasn't just the sudden, commanding tone that startled me.
...Since arriving in this village, I had never once mentioned that I was from Tokyo.
Well... she might have guessed I was from the Kanto region based on my speech patterns...@k...What was I getting so rattled about...?
...It was the sudden, total transformation of the girl's demeanor -- there was no other word for it.
``...You should go back to Tokyo immediately.@k@vS05/27/100500062. ...Otherwise, you will come to regret it terribly.``
The girl... had been holding on to my sleeve the entire time.
She'd been tugging at it to drag me over here, eager to show me this stunning view as quickly as possible... and had held on ever since.
So this had to be the same adorable Rika-chan I had spent the whole day with.
...There was no way an identical stranger could have taken her place without my noticing.
But... even though she'd been gripping my sleeve the entire time... this girl was... someone other than Rika-chan. She looked like Rika-chan, and yet she wasn't. She was... a stranger.
That girl told me in a cold, detached voice...
That I would eventually... come to regret ever setting foot in this village from the bottom of my heart.
"Because the sight of that would be so wretched and pitiful.@k@vS05/27/100500064. ...That is why I'm warning you now, while there's still time."
``...Why... would I come to regret it...?``
``...You ask too many questions.``
...I couldn't believe these cold words were coming from Rika-chan's mouth.
...They couldn't possibly be coming from her.
...Was someone impersonating her... speaking through her like a ventriloquist...?
I wanted to look around, but...@k...her eyes had pinned me in place. I couldn't move.
``...When you were a child standing in the middle of a crosswalk with the light against you, did your parents wait until they finished explaining why it was dangerous before pulling you back?``

``They would pull you back first, wouldn't they?@k@vS05/27/100500068. They'd drag you back to the sidewalk, and only then explain why it was dangerous, right?@k@vS05/27/100500069. ...That's essentially what this is.``
...The Furude Rika I had spent the day with... did not speak like this.
She spoke in short, childish phrases, expressing her emotions honestly... in a charmingly age-appropriate way.
...She would never... speak with such biting cynicism...

``......I've given you my warning.@k@vS05/27/100500071. ...Don't misunderstand -- I'm not saying this because I dislike you.@k@vS05/27/100500072. ...There's no point warning someone you wouldn't mind seeing dead.``
``...Who... are you?@k@vS05/13/101300235. ...You're not... Rika-chan.``
The moment I denied she was Rika-chan... she let out a quiet, stifled laugh, as if she found the whole thing deeply amusing.
...That laugh was not one that belonged to a child her age.
...It was as though she'd been... possessed by something... uncanny.
That the thought even crossed my mind showed just how complete her transformation was.
Had Rika-chan been seized by some terrifying phenomenon?@k...Such occult imaginings spilled forth without the slightest hesitation.
Something is wrong with Rika-chan... someone... help...!
I was about to shout exactly that when I turned toward the mayor.
...And immediately realized he was watching us with a smile.
...Of course... to him, it looked like nothing more than me and Rika-chan playing around.
He hadn't noticed that something terrifying was happening to this girl.
``...Akasaka, you scaredy-cat.@k@vS05/27/100500076. ...Hee hee hee hee hee...!``
The girl had clearly sensed that I was afraid.
...And upon seeing that, she called me a coward... and laughed again.
That cackling girl, laughing while twisting her body in a strange way... lost her balance and toppled over with a thud.
...Rika-chan had fallen.
If this had happened just moments ago, I would have reached out to help her up without a second thought.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...But extending a hand to this ominous girl who looked like Rika-chan but wasn't... required a bit more courage than I could muster.
The girl... cried out thus.
She squirmed upright, then patted the dust off her clothes.
...After that, she looked around with wide, bewildered eyes... wearing a blank expression.
...You're joking... right?@k She was just messing with me...?
If she was going to act like that...@k...it was as though... something had taken her over... and she had lost all memory of what happened during that time...
I don't know if my words reached Rika-chan.@k...She just let out that small sound, as if questioning herself.
``Hey, mister~!@k@vS05/00/oldC00002. Rika-chama too~~!@k@vS05/00/oldC00003. Tea is ready~~!@k@vS05/00/oldC00004. We have salted daifuku too, so please come have some~~!!``
An old woman in a cooking apron was waving energetically from the entrance of the office.
Hearing that there were daifuku, Rika-chan's expression lit up.
``...Yaaaaaay~!``
A carefree smile.
...That was the familiar smile I knew well -- the one belonging to the girl who embodied my ideal daughter.
Any trace of the eerie girl who had been spouting ominous words just moments ago... had vanished completely.
``Come on, Rika-chama~!@k@vS05/00/oldC00006. Go wash your hands first~~!!@k@vS05/00/oldC00007. There's no manjuu for dirty little hands~~!``
``...I'll wash them properly~~!``
``Miiiiii!!``
Rika-chan replied with gusto and ran off toward the washing station beside the office.
She stopped partway, turned around, and called out to me.
``...If Akasaka doesn't hurry, I'll eat his share of manjuu too, and I'll be very pleased with myself.``
``...People who don't wash their hands aren't allowed to eat manjuu, you know?``
...No matter how you looked at it... this was Rika-chan.
Her words, her way of speaking... no matter how you listened, they were unmistakably Rika-chan's.
``Come on now, mister~~~!@k@vS05/00/oldC00009. You're the adult, so set a good example~~!!``
The old woman urged me to go wash my hands too.
...Without resisting, I lined up beside Rika-chan at the washing station.
...Splash, splash.
...I stared at Rika-chan as she washed her hands meticulously, following a procedure she must have learned at school.
``...Huh... wrong... you mean...``
``The way I wash my hands.@k@vS05/27/100500087. ...I only got four gold stickers for it.@k@vS05/27/100500088. ...I'm probably bad at washing my hands.``
``N-no, you're not doing anything wrong.@k@vS05/13/101300241. If you're washing them that thoroughly, that's more than good enough...``
``...That's not true, Akasaka.@k@vS05/27/100500090. You have to use a brush and clean under your fingernails too, or you won't get a gold sticker.``
``Mm... hmm.@k@vS05/13/101300243. ...You're so diligent, Rika-chan... that's really admirable...``
``...Nipah~~~☆ Akasaka praised me.``
No matter how I looked at her... she was Rika-chan.@k...There was no trace whatsoever of that other girl.
That ominous girl from before... what on earth... had that been...?
``...Rika-chan... what did that mean... just now...?``
``...Just now, you told me... to go back to Tokyo... didn't you?``
``...Did I say that?``
...I was lost for words.
...She had no memory of what that other girl had said.
...How could I possibly believe... that something so occult... had actually happened...?
That Rika-chan had been possessed by some eerie girl... who spouted disturbing words through her... I couldn't bring myself to believe it...
``...Rika-chan... you definitely said it.``
Her expression told me that... even after hearing my claim, she truly had no idea what I was talking about.
...She wasn't feigning ignorance or anything of the sort... she genuinely... didn't know.
...What on earth... had that been...?
Following Rika-chan's lead... I, too, let a look of bewilderment settle across my face.
...The two of us, both wearing expressions of utter confusion... must have been quite a comical sight.
While being treated to tea and salted daifuku inside the office... I was made to sit through an endless propaganda video of the resistance movement shot by the Defense Alliance.
changeReason: New translation renders 死守同盟 as 'Defense Alliance' instead of 'Guardians,' better conveying the activist nature of the group.

TextENGNew: The old men passionately held forth about how grueling and protracted the battle against the dam had been... but their words didn't reach me.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...Having heard these stories countless times before, Rika-chan was drowsily nodding off... and I found myself watching only her the entire time.
Eventually, Rika-chan noticed me staring and smiled at me while rubbing her eyes.
...That smile was unmistakably Rika-chan's... without even a hint of that sinister shadow.
...After that, my irrational fear of Rika-chan gradually faded with time... but I could never forget what had happened.
It eventually grew dark. The mayor and his people offered to prepare dinner, but I declined, saying I would come back tomorrow.
...I had to return to the hotel and make my routine report to the section chief.
``I see.@k@vS05/00/104100083. ...Well then, please come again tomorrow!@k@vS05/00/104100084. Just phone us and we'll send someone right over to pick you up.``
``I'm grateful for your hospitality.@k@vS05/13/101300249. I'd love to come again tomorrow.``
``Please do!@k@vS05/00/104100086. Rika-chama seems to have taken quite a liking to you!``
``...Akasaka, are you coming again tomorrow?@k@vS05/27/100500096. I finish after fifth period, so if you could come around three o'clock, that would make me happy.``
...Please come again.@kThat innocent smile implored me.
...Not a trace of that sinister aura remained.
...Gradually... I began to wonder if what had happened had simply been some kind of misunderstanding... a trick of my imagination...
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: If I thought about it rationally...

...Being liked by Rika-chan, daughter of the Furude household and one of the Three Families... was a significant foothold for my investigation going forward.@kIt was like being handed a pass to enter Hinamizawa freely.

If I could just convince myself that the earlier incident was nothing but my imagination... then having this girl -- the very embodiment of my ideal daughter -- take a liking to me... was anything but a bad thing...

I had successfully wormed my way into the inner circle of the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance, earning a warm welcome from the village chief and other key figures.@k...It should have been a tremendous achievement.
And yet... my heart remained unsettled.
...Not until I returned to the hotel... and forced down an almost painfully cold beer to clear my head.@k...The shock of that incomprehensible, uncanny incident continued to gnaw at me...
